p.em: Scenario: Reader closes → Kernel generates EPIPE/SIGPIPE → Writer terminates

div.p: SIGPIPE provides an asynchronous backpressure mechanism. Without it, a writer would block indefinitely if the reader exited or would continue to waste system resources generating unconsumed data.

ul.li:
pipe_inode_info:
readers: unsigned int (Offset 0x24) -> 0
writers: unsigned int (Offset 0x28) -> 1

IF pipe->readers == 0:
SET thread->errno = EPIPE
SEND SIGPIPE to current
RETURN -1
